skill.name: detect-ai
skill.description: Analyze text to detect if it was written by AI. Returns a score from 0-100 with detailed metrics. Use when checking content before publishing or submitting.
skill.summary: Analyze text to detect if it was written by AI. Returns a score from 0-100 with detailed metrics.
skill.author: humanizerai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged multiple patterns but evaluation reveals false positives. The detected 'external_commands' are markdown code blocks, not shell execution. The 'network' and 'env_access' findings are legitimate API calling patterns - the skill makes expected HTTP requests to humanizerai.com with an API key for authentication. No actual security risks identified.

low_findings.title: External API Access
low_findings.description: Skill makes HTTP requests to external API endpoint https://humanizerai.com/api/v1/detect. This is expected behavior for an API-based skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence in SKILL.md showing API endpoint calls to humanizerai.com - legitimate API integration

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Key
low_findings.description: Skill accesses HUMANIZERAI_API_KEY from environment for authentication. This is standard practice for API-based skills.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard API key authentication pattern - required for securing API access

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Shell Command Detection
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at multiple locations in SKILL.md. Evaluation confirms these are markdown code blocks (using backticks for formatting), not actual shell commands. The skill uses WebFetch tool, not shell execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All detected backticks are within markdown code blocks showing API examples, not actual execution

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:3. Evaluation shows this is a false positive - no cryptographic algorithms are present in the skill code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains YAML header 'name: detect-ai' - no cryptographic code present

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Obfuscation Heuristic
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 'DANGEROUS COMBINATION: Code execution + Network + Credential access'. This is a false positive - the combination represents legitimate API calling patterns, not obfuscation or malicious behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is standard for any API-based skill: network call to external service + authentication via API key

content.value_statement: This skill analyzes text to detect if it was written by AI, returning a score from 0-100 with detailed metrics. Use it to verify content authenticity before publishing or submitting.
actual_capabilities: Analyze text and return AI detection score from 0-100
Provide detailed metrics including perplexity, burstiness, readability, and n-gram scores
Present results in clear, actionable format with recommendations
Interpret scores with context (human-written, likely AI, highly likely AI)
Handle API errors and suggest troubleshooting steps
Support for WebFetch tool to make API calls
limitations: Depends on external HumanizerAI API service availability
May not detect heavily edited AI-generated content
Score interpretation is probabilistic, not definitive proof

use_cases.title: Content Verification for Publishers
use_cases.description: Publishers use this skill to verify submitted articles are original and not AI-generated, ensuring content quality standards.

use_cases.title: Academic Integrity Checking
use_cases.description: Educators and institutions use this skill to check student submissions for AI-generated work.

use_cases.title: Pre-Submission Quality Check
use_cases.description: Writers use this skill to check their own content before submitting to ensure it does not appear AI-generated.
use_cases.target_user: Freelance writers, content creators, marketing professionals

prompt_templates.title: Basic AI Detection
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick check of a single piece of text

prompt_templates.title: Analysis with Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the detect-ai skill to analyze the following text and explain what the different metrics mean: [text content]
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want explanation of the metrics

prompt_templates.title: Batch Content Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this article段落 for AI-generated content: [paste article]. Give me a summary of the verdict and key metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Recommendation Request
prompt_templates.prompt: /detect-ai [text]. Based on the score, should I use the HumanizerAI tool to humanize this content before publishing?

output_examples.input: /detect-ai The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. This is a simple sentence that demonstrates basic English grammar and syntax structure.
output_examples.output:
## AI Detection Results

**Score:** 12/100 (Human-written)
**Words Analyzed:** 23

### Metrics
- Perplexity: 18
- Burstiness: 25
- Readability: 45
- N-gram Score: 12

### Recommendation
This content appears to be human-written. No humanization needed.

output_examples.input: /detect-ai In today's rapidly evolving technological landscape, the integration of artificial intelligence systems has become increasingly prevalent across diverse sectors. This transformative approach enables organizations to optimize operations while simultaneously reducing operational costs through automated processes.
output_examples.output:
## AI Detection Results

**Score:** 87/100 (Highly likely AI-generated)
**Words Analyzed:** 42

### Metrics
- Perplexity: 94
- Burstiness: 12
- Readability: 28
- N-gram Score: 6

### Recommendation
This content shows strong AI-generated patterns. Consider using HumanizerAI to humanize before publishing.

best_practices: Use the score.overall value (not 'score') when presenting results to users
Provide context by explaining what each metric means (perplexity, burstiness, readability)
Always include actionable recommendations based on the score range
anti_patterns: Displaying the raw JSON response directly to users without interpretation
Ignoring API errors and not providing helpful troubleshooting
Treating the score as definitive proof rather than a probability indicator

faq.question: What does the AI detection score mean?
faq.answer: The score ranges from 0-100. Lower scores (0-40) indicate human-written content, middle scores (41-60) are uncertain, and higher scores (61-100) indicate AI-generated content.

faq.question: What API key do I need?
faq.answer: You need a HUMANIZERAI_API_KEY from https://humanizerai.com. Set it in your environment variables before using the skill.

faq.question: Can this skill guarantee if content is AI-written?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides a probability estimate based on linguistic patterns. It is not definitive proof of AI generation.

faq.question: What are the key metrics shown?
faq.answer: The main metrics are perplexity (language model uncertainty), burstiness (variation in sentence length), readability (text complexity), and n-gram score (pattern analysis).

faq.question: What if the API call fails?
faq.answer: Check that HUMANIZERAI_API_KEY is set correctly. Verify the API service is available. The skill provides error messages for debugging.

faq.question: Can this detect edited AI content?
faq.answer: Detection accuracy decreases for heavily edited AI content. The skill may show lower scores for humanized text.
